regimeSub: June CPI at 3.5% YoY below estimates extends soft inflation print; BOK hike cycle and TSMC capex lift Asia risk.
themes: June CPI 3.5% YoY below est (energy eased per CNBC) → NY Fed Williams 'inflation peaked' call validated, July FOMC hike odds fade further despite Dallas Fed Logan's hawkish pushback
BOK hike to 2.75% (first in 3.5 yrs) now fully digested → USDKRW 1481 stable, KOSPI 6578 holds gains as AI chip demand underpins SK Hynix/Samsung valuations
TSMC raises capex + revenue guidance on AI chip demand (Thu) → Korea growth forecast 3%, HBM supply crisis narrative extends through 2027, semi momentum intact
Brent $90 (+6% from last week) as renewed Hormuz hostilities (CNBC) and Trump Iran sanctions push offset OPEC+ August quota hike → gold $4025 at highs on geopolitical hedge

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10Y 4.57% minus CPI 3.5% = +107bp real; restrictive but narrowing as disinflation persists

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2Y 4.16% / 10Y 4.57% = +41bp; curve steeper on fiscal concerns, BOJ taper endpoint in view

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads stable as retail resilience (Axios: 'spending while grumbling') keeps default fears contained

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.77 subdued despite Hormuz flare-up; equity vol not pricing tail risk from Iran escalation

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $90.07 bid as renewed Hormuz hostilities (CNBC) collide with OPEC+ Aug quota hike; geopolitical premium sticky

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4025.9 ATH as Trump Iran sanctions push (Crypto Briefing) + real rate compression drive safe haven bid

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1481 stable post-BOK hike; won strength capped as Fed/BOK policy divergence narrows on US disinflation

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: TSMC $100bn US capex + raised guidance validates AI capex cycle; HBM supply crisis thesis intact thru 2027

headline: June CPI undershoot to 3.5% anchors Fed hold narrative while BOK kicks off hike cycle; Hormuz tensions keep Brent at $90 and gold at record highs despite equity calm.
